todaysEvents.summary: Iran's IRGC seizes MSC Francesca and Epaminondas in Strait of Hormuz (Apr 22), citing unauthorized transit and navigation tampering; Greek-owned Euphoria also stranded on Iranian shores. Seizures executed hours after Trump extends ceasefire indefinitely — national security adviser: extension 'has no meaning'; 3 Western commercial vessels now in Iranian custody as Touska counter-leverage (CNBC, NBC, Al Jazeera, Washington Post, Euronews, Apr 22).
todaysEvents.delta: Escalation: Iran executes kinetic counter-measure post-ceasefire extension; MSC ship seizures signal Iran treating extension as operationally 'meaningless' — new escalation vector; tail risk rises to 40%

todaysEvents.summary: Malaysia energy emergency: PM Anwar confirms Petronas now a net fuel importer — exporting premium crude (~350k bpd), importing heavy Gulf crude for domestic refining (~700k bpd need). RON95 quota cut 300L→200L; civil service WFH from Apr 15; unsubsidised petrol RM3.27/L (+68 sen). April/May supply secured; June uncertain per Economy Minister (The Star, paultan.org, Bernama, Apr 4-6).
todaysEvents.delta: Escalation: Malaysia's 'windfall exporter' narrative inverted — Petronas structural import dependency exposes Malaysia to sustained Hormuz disruption; June supply gap is new ASEAN demand-side risk

todaysEvents.summary: Brent $101.73 confirmed (+3.3% Apr 22 close, TradingEconomics/Fortune) on IRGC ship seizures; TTF €42.39 confirmed (Apr 22 OilPriceAPI); JKM $15.81 confirmed (+3.77% Apr 21, TradingEconomics); D52 Brent ~$102.50 est. (+0.8%); iTraxx Asia IG ~198bp est. (+8bp); ASEAN HY ~575bp (+15bp); tail risk 40% (Apr 23).
todaysEvents.delta: Market re-escalation: Brent reverses D51 ceasefire-relief pullback; IRGC ship seizures reprice disruption risk upward; iTraxx widening resumes on 40% tail probability

scenarios.label: Base
scenarios.description: Pakistan-mediated framework survives IRGC ship seizures — Iran uses MSC Francesca + Epaminondas as negotiating leverage, not ceasefire termination. Unified Iranian proposal emerges; vessels returned in exchange for partial blockade modification; nuclear framework accepted with face-saving language. Brent retreats toward $88-95/bbl; iTraxx Asia IG tightens below 175bp. Requires Iran to stand down kinetic posture — narrow (10%) given seizures executed hours after the ceasefire extension, but Pakistani back-channel still technically open.

scenarios.label: Stress
scenarios.description: Dominant scenario — IRGC ship seizures (MSC Francesca, Epaminondas, Euphoria) become part of a kinetic stalemate, not ceasefire collapse. Iran holds vessels as Touska counter-leverage; US maintains blockade; Pakistan-brokered back-channel continues inconclusively. Hormuz remains contested: selective bilateral transit; IRGC periodic seizures and harassment sustained. Brent stabilises $95-115/bbl; JKM $16-22/MMBtu; iTraxx Asia IG 190-215bp. Malaysia June supply gap remains live risk; Ras Laffan restart not before late Aug 2026.

scenarios.label: Tail
scenarios.description: IRGC ship seizures escalate into ceasefire collapse — US responds to MSC Francesca + Epaminondas seizure with force; Iran retaliates against US naval asset; formal ceasefire terminated. IRGC activates Gulf-wide blockade; Kharg Island seizure or Aramco targeting brings GCC production under acute threat. Brent spikes $130-170/bbl; LNG markets seize; Malaysia June supply gap becomes July-August crisis. Tail at 40% (up from 33% D51) reflecting Iran's kinetic escalation despite ceasefire extension — seizures hours after extension signal IRGC treats extension as operationally void.
